skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate vocal songs, instrumentals, jingles, and background tracks with ElevenLabs Music through the RunComfy CLI. The skill helps structure prompts, lyrics, durations, instrumental settings, output formats, and expected CLI behavior for music generation.
skill.summary: Generate structured AI music tracks with ElevenLabs Music through RunComfy.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks, weak-crypto strings, and documentation URLs, but review found no malicious code or prompt-injection attempt. The real risks are intended use of the RunComfy CLI, outbound RunComfy API calls, generated file downloads, and local token handling, so publication is reasonable with a medium-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Intended External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run the local runcomfy CLI for music generation. This is expected behavior, but it still executes an external command and depends on the installed CLI and its argument handling.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command use is explicit and central to the skill. Context shows a legitimate RunComfy invocation rather than arbitrary shell execution.

medium_findings.title: Outbound API Use and Generated Downloads
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts and lyrics to RunComfy model services and downloads generated audio. Users should treat prompt text and lyrics as data shared with an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly documents remote API submission, polling, and audio download behavior. The endpoints are expected for the skill but create privacy and network exposure.

low_findings.title: Local Token Storage Guidance
low_findings.description: The documentation states that runcomfy login stores an API token under the user configuration directory with restrictive permissions. This is a normal credential pattern, but mishandling the token could expose account access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and mode are explicitly documented. The text includes reasonable handling guidance and does not request token exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak-Crypto Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Most static findings come from Markdown inline code, fenced shell examples, model names, durations, or documentation text. Review found no Ruby backtick execution and no weak cryptographic algorithm implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious tokens are visible in prose and examples, not executable Ruby or crypto code. The command substitution text is defensive documentation about shell injection boundaries.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill relies on runcomfy command execution for core behavior. Agents should keep execution limited to documented runcomfy commands and avoid arbitrary shell composition.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command pattern is directly shown in the skill examples. It is legitimate, but external command execution remains a meaningful risk factor.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Service Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses RunComfy hosted services for model execution and generated audio retrieval. This requires outbound network access and external processing of user prompts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The remote API behavior is explicitly documented. No hidden endpoint or callback was found in the reviewed file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Storage Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references local RunComfy API token storage and an environment variable option for CI. Users should avoid placing tokens in prompts, logs, or committed files.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file names a specific token path and safe permission mode. The risk is operational credential exposure, not confirmed malicious access.

content.user_title: Generate Music with ElevenLabs on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Teams need quick custom music without managing a full audio production workflow. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through structured ElevenLabs Music prompts using the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Builds prompts for full vocal songs with section markers such as intro, verse, chorus, bridge, and outro.
Creates instrumental track requests by using the force_instrumental option and clear no-vocal wording.
Sets music duration from 5 seconds to 5 minutes through the RunComfy model input.
Explains output format choices and where the RunComfy CLI saves downloaded audio files.
Provides examples for jingles, podcast intros, game loops, multilingual lyrics, and longer songs.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a valid RunComfy account, and network access to RunComfy services.
Does not generate sound effects, text-to-speech, voice cloning, or singer identity cloning.
Does not verify copyright ownership for lyrics, melodies, brand assets, or reference material.
Longer tracks cost more because pricing scales with generated audio duration.

use_cases.title: Produce podcast intro music
use_cases.description: Create a short instrumental intro or outro with a clean ending and consistent brand mood.

use_cases.title: Draft campaign jingles
use_cases.description: Generate short brand stingers or vocal jingles from a style brief, duration, and simple lyric structure.

use_cases.title: Create game background loops
use_cases.description: Request loop-friendly instrumental tracks with stable tempo, clear instrumentation, and target scene mood.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner instrumental track
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 30 second instrumental track for a calm product demo. Use warm piano, soft percussion, and no vocals. Save it to the default output folder.

prompt_templates.title: Structured vocal song
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a short song with sections
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 60 second upbeat pop song with intro, verse, chorus, bridge, and outro sections. Use the lyrics I provide and keep the vocal style clear.

prompt_templates.title: Brand jingle iteration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three 5 to 10 second jingle directions for a friendly software brand. Make each version instrumental first and summarize the style differences.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced multilingual release
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare related tracks in multiple languages
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 45 second song in the same style for English and Spanish lyrics. Keep tempo, structure, instruments, and chorus energy consistent across both versions.

output_examples.input: A creator asks for a 20 second podcast intro with no vocals.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a concise instrumental prompt, sets a short duration, enables instrumental output, and explains where the downloaded audio will appear.

output_examples.input: A marketer asks for a cheerful 5 second brand stinger.
output_examples.output: The assistant turns the request into a brief jingle direction with bright instruments, a clean ending, and a low-cost draft duration.

output_examples.input: A game developer asks for a loop-friendly forest exploration track.
output_examples.output: The assistant specifies mood, tempo, repeating groove, no vocals, and a duration suitable for testing in a game scene.

best_practices: Start with a short draft before generating a full-length track.
State genre, mood, tempo, key instruments, vocal need, and section structure in one clear prompt.
Confirm rights for supplied lyrics or brand material before generating commercial music.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API tokens, private lyrics, or confidential campaign text into prompts without approval.
Do not combine contradictory styles in one generation request.
Do not use this skill for sound effects, voice cloning, or text-to-speech tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill generate the audio locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the RunComfy CLI to send the request to RunComfy services and download the generated audio.

faq.question: Can it make instrumental music only?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses the instrumental option and also adds clear no-vocal wording to the prompt.

faq.question: What track lengths are supported?
faq.answer: The documented model range is 5 seconds to 5 minutes per generation request.

faq.question: Can it clone a specific singer voice?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not expose voice cloning or specific singer identity controls.

faq.question: Is it suitable for commercial projects?
faq.answer: The skill describes the output as commercial-friendly, but users remain responsible for rights in supplied lyrics or references.

faq.question: What setup is required?
faq.answer: Users need the RunComfy CLI, authentication through RunComfy, and outbound network access to RunComfy endpoints.
